phase_name: Cancel PRs and restore MRF to processing area

remaining_tasks.name: Add recallMRF() function with dual-path logic
Add blockedMRFIds cache, showMRFContextMenu(), and wire to MRF list rows

decisions.decision: Trigger via right-click context menu on MRF rows, not action bar buttons
decisions.rationale: User requirement — consistent with existing PO ID right-click pattern for RFP creation

decisions.decision: Two-path recall: simple cancel (all PRs Pending/Rejected) vs force recall (Finance-Approved PRs with POs at Pending Procurement)
decisions.rationale: Option B chosen by user — covers both pre-Finance and post-Finance scenarios in one action

decisions.decision: Block recall entirely if any linked PO is Procuring/Procured/Delivered
decisions.rationale: User requirement — safety net to prevent cancellation of in-progress procurement

decisions.decision: Safety net at context menu level: blockedMRFIds Set suppresses menu before user can click
decisions.rationale: UX safety net — context menu simply does not appear for blocked MRFs, preventing confusion

decisions.decision: refreshBlockedMRFs() queries pos by mrf_id with compound where clause, not by pr_id per PR
decisions.rationale: System efficiency — N reads instead of N×M reads per snapshot cycle; server-side filtering avoids transferring non-blocking PO documents

decisions.decision: Void approved POs by setting procurement_status: 'Cancelled', not deleting
decisions.rationale: Preserves audit trail; deletion is irreversible and removes history

decisions.decision: Finance-approval recall flow added to BACKLOG.md (not this phase)
decisions.rationale: Phase 70 delivers immediate unblocking. Full recall-with-Finance-approval is a separate workflow to design alongside the Finance Pending Approvals tab

next_action: Run /gsd:execute-phase 70 after /clear — execute Task 1 (recallMRF function) then Task 2 (blockedMRFIds cache + showMRFContextMenu + MRF row wiring)
context_notes: Plan is fully refined and verification-passed. All design decisions are locked. The plan went through 2 checker iterations: first pass had missing VALIDATION.md and a must_haves gap; both fixed. Then user requested 3 revisions post-verification: (1) right-click trigger instead of action bar buttons, (2) two-path Option B with procurement-progress edge case, (3) safety net suppressing the context menu entirely for blocked MRFs. Final efficiency improvement: refreshBlockedMRFs uses mrf_id compound query instead of pr_id loop — reduces N×M Firestore reads to N reads per snapshot cycle.
